todaysPlan.activity: Homework Spelling – Schwa And High-Frequency Words
todaysPlan.reason: Active uploaded homework is "Benchmark Advance Spelling Unit 9 Week 2", so today's required work must anchor on Schwa sound (unstressed vowel), Long vowel sounds (long i, long o, long a), High-frequency sight words, Antonyms before broader profile goals.
todaysPlan.method: Read the assignment-aligned content, ask evidence-based checks, then use profile motivators only as framing.
probeSequence: Read or preview the assignment content
Ask why it happened using text evidence
Have the child explain the concept back

childProfile: Reina is an 8-year-old academically advanced wrestler who thrives on challenge, competition, and speed. She's bilingual (English/Japanese), scores 100% on spelling tests, is a year ahead of grade level, and gets frustrated when work is too easy—not when it's too hard. She loves wrestling metaphors, celebrates in Japanese (すごい, やった), and wants to be pushed. Her competitive nature drives her: streaks, personal bests, scoreboards land perfectly. She has strong pattern recognition (caught comparative adjectives instantly), championship-level addition fluency (100% accuracy), and explicit self-advocacy (asked for multiplication/division 9 weeks ago). She disengages from mechanical drill (worksheet coins 26% accuracy) but masters applied contexts (store-game 86%) and competitive games (Wheel of Fortune 1,300-600 victory). Match her fast pace, acknowledge her intelligence, never baby-talk, and honor her self-identified growth zones. She's discovering her innate talents—every session should challenge her ceiling, not her floor.
stopAfter: After the active homework check is complete, or earlier if frustration is rising.
rewardPolicy: Reward only after the active homework is checked; broader review goals belong in review mode.
